mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) signal expanding consumer demand that feeds directly into corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is a continuous trend signal with no activation threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month momentum and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls below 0.25% (effective zero lower bound), the policy rate signals an extraordinary easing regime in which investors reallocate from cash to equities in anticipation of prolonged accommodation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16 in snapshot indicates zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following FEDFUNDS < 0.25% readings are not significantly higher than the unconditional historical mean (t-test p > 0.05), or hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous occurrence of elevated UNRATE (above 7%) combined with near-zero FEDFUNDS indicates a monetary authority actively counteracting a labor market shock; the joint signal captures policy-driven risk-on repricing that a single series alone would miss, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price forward recovery ahead of realized labor improvement.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=8.3 and FEDFUNDS=0.16 co-occur in snapshot
failure_condition: Joint UNRATE/FEDFUNDS trigger produces negative or flat median 20d forward returns in more than 45% of historical triggered instances, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over univariate FEDFUNDS model.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO signals reopening of production capacity and rebuilding of supply chains, which markets price as durable earnings recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is muted or absent outside of sharp contraction-to-recovery transitions.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=98.04 with concurrent high UNRATE suggests early recovery phase
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive correlation with 3-month INDPRO change (Rank IC < 0.03) during identified post-shock recovery windows in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) provide a fast-reacting confirmation of labor market stabilization that short-horizon traders price into equities within days, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. This is a continuous always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX return sign matches PAYEMS month-over-month sign in less than 52% of out-of-sample observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 900 thousand units signals contraction in interest-rate-sensitive residential investment, foreshadowing broader demand weakness that pressures equity earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once the threshold is breached.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=760 in snapshot is below the 900k threshold
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following HOUST < 900k readings are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or show positive median return over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of low FEDFUNDS with rising CPIAUCSL implies a deeply negative real policy rate, which mechanically incentivizes flows out of cash and fixed income into equities as investors seek inflation-protected real returns, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. Neither series alone captures this real-rate effect.
failure_condition: Estimated real rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY inflation) shows no significant negative correlation with subsequent 60d SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of extreme labor market distress (UNRATE above 8%) coincident with a contraction in real GDP (GDPC1 declining quarter-over-quarter), risk-off sentiment dominates short-horizon equity pricing and forward returns skew negative over the next 5 trading days, reversing the typical countercyclical equity rally seen in milder downturns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns during UNRATE>8% and GDPC1 contraction periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of such triggered instances.
